mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and reduces borrowing costs for firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month FEDFUNDS change and 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns are not significantly different from unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days once the threshold is crossed.
failure_condition: Following triggered instances, 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or Rank IC conditional on trigger is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where PAYEMS growth decelerates while UNRATE simultaneously ticks up indicates a genuine labor market turning point rather than noise, and this joint signal produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days because it precedes rapid repricing of growth expectations.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-deceleration and UNRATE-increase signal shows Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or its predictive power is not statistically distinguishable from either single-series signal alone (interaction term insignificant at p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle (elevated FEDFUNDS), a decline in HOUST reflects interest-rate-sensitive demand destruction in housing that spreads to broader consumer confidence and durable goods spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; the same HOUST decline in an easing regime is not expected to carry the same signal.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS > 4.0% and HOUST falls over 3 months, 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the regime-conditional Rank IC is not higher than the unconditional HOUST-return Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent uptrend in INDPRO over the trailing 6 months signals expanding industrial capacity utilization and corporate output, which supports forward earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as an always-on trend signal.
failure_condition: Rank IC between 6-month INDPRO trend and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or the sign of the relationship reverses in two consecutive non-overlapping 2-year subsamples.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS month-over-month growth falls below zero for two consecutive readings, it signals a contraction in consumer spending that leads corporate revenue guidance cuts, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days once the threshold condition is met.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth negative for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Following triggered instances, 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or conditional Rank IC is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS remains elevated while CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation decelerates, the implied real policy rate rises, tightening financial conditions faster than nominal rates suggest and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprice the effective tightening.
failure_condition: The implied real-rate-rise signal (FEDFUNDS flat/up combined with CPIAUCSL YoY deceleration) shows Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or is statistically indistinguishable from the FEDFUNDS-only signal (interaction term p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, deceleration of PAYEMS month-over-month gains below their trailing 12-month average signals fading labor demand momentum that historically precedes broader economic slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; the same deceleration in an early-cycle recovery regime does not carry this negative signal.
failure_condition: In periods identified as late-cycle expansion (UNRATE near multi-year lows), PAYEMS deceleration is followed by positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances, or regime-conditional Rank IC does not exceed the unconditional Rank IC.
